title: Postmortem - Incident #8856: Postgres Primary Disk I/O Saturation
content:
Postmortem - Incident #8856: Postgres Primary Disk I/O Saturation

### Incident Summary
On June 1, database write latencies rose past 800ms, causing database connection timeouts. The application was severely degraded.

### Root Cause
A batch analytics job running at peak time triggered heavy unindexed joins. This caused excessive disk read/write swapping on the primary server storage.

### Resolution
Identified the rogue query execution PID using pg_stat_activity. Terminated the batch process, and applied an index on the join column (transaction_id). Write latency immediately normalized below 10ms.

### Action Items
- Disable analytics scripts on the production primary DB; redirect all analytical reads to the read-replica.
- Implement disk IO alert threshold at 85% utilization.
